plainEnglish: At Level AAA, users can postpone or suppress interruptions—popups, toast notifications, auto-updates, chat nudges—except genuine emergencies. Unexpected interruptions disrupt people who are concentrating or using assistive technology.
whyItMatters: Users with cognitive disabilities, and those using screen readers, lose their place and focus when notifications or auto-updates interrupt them unexpectedly.
quickCheck: Confirm users can mute or defer notifications and auto-updates, and check there's no <meta http-equiv="refresh"> (axe meta-refresh-no-exceptions).
commonMistakes: Toast notifications or modals that pop up unprompted with no way to mute them.
Using <meta http-equiv="refresh"> to reload content (axe meta-refresh-no-exceptions).
Pushing live updates that steal focus or shift content while the user is reading.

codeExamples.label: Respect a user preference before interrupting
Unconditional, unsuppressable interruptions
codeExamples.code:
// Pops up every 10s with no way to suppress
setInterval(() => showToast("New activity!"), 10000);
